page: Amazon Web Services c6a.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

The processor supports AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 support. In benchmark evaluations, the single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% with a stress-ng score of 2985.49. However, multi-core performance is weak, scoring in the bottom 25% in stress-ng and bottom 10% in Geekbench multi-core tests. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, perform in the average range.

Memory performance is a notable constraint, with Passmark memory scores and cached read-only memory bandwidth falling into the bottom 10%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting the limitations of the 4.0 GB memory capacity and lack of local NVMe storage. Given these characteristics, the instance is suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, or small-scale text processing, rather than memory-intensive databases or parallelized compilation workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng benchmarks. However, its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are limited, with both Geekbench multi-core and Passmark memory scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. The instance lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. It supports hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI but lacks AVX-512 instructions. Given the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak multi-threaded performance, the c6a.large is best suited for single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, and lightweight text processing workloads rather than memory-intensive databases or parallel processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 4 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance and dedicated vCPUs for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz with 2 dedicated vCPUs.
4.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 2.0 GB per core allocation.
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks.
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.
Suited for single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and text processing.
